$comment: Machine-readable Lattice chain manifest. Story #2057. Walked by tests/lib/lattice-chain-closure.test.ts. Validated against docs/lattice-chains.md by lattice-self-maintenance.test.ts.

chains.title: Behavior Aggregation Cascade (BEH-AGG-001 + ADAPT-BEH-001)
chains.description: Per-call BEH-* CallScore → rolled-up behavior_profile:* CallerAttribute → ADAPT-BEH-001 CallerTarget upsert → composed prompt section. ADAPT leg closed by story #2074 (Phase 1 = 3 of 9 buckets: companion / supervision / engagement).

links.outputNotes: Phase 1 covers 3 of 9 BEH-AGG-001 buckets (companion / supervision / engagement). Deliberately avoids BEH-EMPATHY-RATE + BEH-PROACTIVE (owned by ADAPT-PERS-001). Remaining 6 buckets (personality / curriculum / learning / reinforcement / onboarding / core_style) deferred to Phase 2.

Emitted as 'Companion profile signals' / 'Personality profile signals' / etc. sub-sections via behaviorAttrs grouped by namespace prefix. The CallerTarget written by ADAPT (link 3) is read by the targets.ts transform at compose time — see chain `behavior-target-cascade`.

chains.title: Skill EMA Aggregation Cascade (SKILL-AGG-001)
chains.description: Per-call skill_* CallScore → time-decay EMA into CallerTarget.currentScore → ACHIEVE goal progress.

chains.title: Coaching Aggregation Cascade (COACH-AGG-001)
chains.description: Per-call COACH_* CallScore → threshold-mapped CallerAttribute → composed prompt section via quickstart.ts learning_guidance.

chains.title: Per-LO Mastery Cascade (LEARN-ASSESS-001)
chains.description: EXTRACT-stage LEARN-ASSESS-001 (assessmentMode=curriculum_mastery) scores each discussed Learning Objective 0-1 in the call transcript → AGGREGATE writes per-LO mastery to CallerAttribute(key=curriculum:{specSlug}:lo_mastery:{moduleSlug}:{loRef}) via the canonical track-progress writer (monotonic ratchet, slug-canonical, #611 Fix A) → ADAPT-stage `lo_rollup` Goal strategy reads the per-LO rows to update LEARN Goal.progress → COMPOSE reads the same rows via buildLoMasteryMap to emit per-module mastery into the composed prompt. 4 links rather than 5: LO mastery skips the SCORE_AGENT stage (it does not write per-LO CallScore rows; the EXTRACT outputs flow directly in-memory through learningAssessment.outcomes to the AGGREGATE writer).

links.outputNotes: LEARN-ASSESS-001 (specRole=EXTRACT, outputType=LEARN) drives the prompt that asks the model to score each discussed Learning Objective 0-1. The pipeline route's `runLearningAssessment` parses the response into `learningAssessment.outcomes: Record<loRef, number>` and threads it directly to `updateCurriculumProgress({loMastery})` without intermediate CallScore writes. #611 Fix A canonical slug-form is enforced at the AGGREGATE write boundary, not here.

Single chokepoint for per-LO mastery writes. Monotonic ratchet via Math.max(existing, clamped). Slug-canonical: `moduleId` is resolved through `resolveModuleSlug(curriculumId, moduleIdOrName)` (#611 Fix A) before key construction to prevent dual-key drift. Per-Playbook tier cap applied to the contribution. ESLint guard `hf-curriculum/no-bare-module-progress-update` blocks competing writers; `tests/lib/mastery-roundtrip.test.ts` pins the write→read shape.

links.outputKey: Goal.progress (mean of per-LO mastery across modules the goal.ref resolves to)
links.outputNotes: `lo_rollup` is the only valid `Goal.progressStrategy` for LEARN goals tracking per-LO mastery (StrategyKey enum, `no-bare-strategy-key` ESLint rule, #1599). Reader is `deriveLearnGoalProgressFromRef` in `lib/goals/track-progress.ts:419` (suffix-match on `:lo_mastery:<moduleSlug>:<actualLoRef>`). Working-set selection at COMPOSE time also reads `loMasteryMap[moduleId:loRef]` for module choice, but the Goal-strategy reader is the canonical ADAPT-stage consumer.

links.outputKey: per-module mastery section in composed prompt (working-set selection + per-LO thresholds)
links.outputNotes: `buildLoMasteryMap(callerAttributes, specSlug)` in `lib/prompt/composition/lo-mastery-map.ts` returns `Record<moduleSlug:loRef, number>` after filtering CURRICULUM-scope rows by `curriculum:{specSlug}:lo_mastery:` prefix (#928 cross-course bleed prevention). `modules.ts` then threads `loMasteryMap` into the scheduler / working-set-selector to pick LOs whose mastery is below threshold for the next call. COMPOSE re-reads the AGGREGATE-written CallerAttribute rows directly; it does not consume the ADAPT-stage Goal.progress output.

chains.title: Wizard Config Write Invariant (chat-tool merge → COMPOSE-stage reader, #1995)
chains.description: Live IELTS Speaking Practice incident on hf_sandbox 2026-06-18: `Playbook.config.teachingMode = "directive"` (a value from the `interactionPattern` union, not the `teachingMode` union) crashed every new-learner ComposedPrompt build. PR #1993 added read-side defensive fallback; #1995 closes the write-side reuse gap by routing every chat-tool merge site through the runtime type guards in `lib/content-trust/resolve-config.ts`. Five-layer Coverage-pillar closure: source SETs at `lib/wizard/enum-sets.ts`, runtime guards `is*`, ESLint `hf-wizard/no-untyped-enum-write-in-wizard` chokepoint, type narrowing in `PlaybookConfig`, AI tool schema `enum: [...]` arrays, and a ratchet vitest that pins all of the above.

links.outputKey: Playbook.config.{interactionPattern, teachingMode, audience, planEmphasis, lessonPlanModel} via updatePlaybookConfig
links.outputNotes: Sibling writer at _reuse-config-merge.ts; admin-tool-handlers.ts::handleUpdatePlaybookConfig via filterEnumBearingUpdates. All three sites guarded by isTeachingMode / isInteractionPattern / isAudience / isPlanEmphasis / isLessonPlanModel / isFirstCallMode / isProgressionMode from lib/content-trust/resolve-config.ts.

links.outputKey: criticalRules[] entries in composed prompt (the load-bearing read that crashed pre-#1993)
links.outputNotes: Read-side fallback added in PR #1993 (`isTeachingMode(...) ? mode : "recall"`) is defence-in-depth; #1995's write-side guards make the fallback path unreachable in the steady state. Sibling consumers: lib/content-trust/resolve-config.ts::INTENT_PATTERN_OVERRIDES (interactionPattern), lib/prompt/composition/transforms/audience.ts (audience).

chains.title: Generic SessionFocus 4th-layer substrate (CALLER_ATTRIBUTE_NEXT outputType, #2145 / #2154)
chains.description: Per-session learner-facing emphasis label cascade. Skill scores aggregated into CallerTarget.currentScore → CALLER_ATTRIBUTE_NEXT-typed AnalysisSpec routes via session-focus-policy runner → writes CallerAttribute(scope=specSlug, key=session_focus:next_{moduleSlug}) → compose transform reads + emits [SESSION FOCUS] block + PinnedCardContent. Distinct from ADAPT (which writes CallerTarget). Honest empty-state at every link: when no scored inputs exist the runner writes nothing and the transform omits the block.

links.outputNotes: SKILL-AGG-001 ema_to_caller_target writes CallerTarget.currentScore for skill_* parameters. The SessionFocus runner reads these scores to pick the weakest.

links.consumesKey: CallerTarget.currentScore (scope-based via spec.config.inputSkills pattern — runner reads `where: {callerId, parameterId: {in: config.inputSkills}}`; specific parameter ids live in each spec.json's config, not in the generic runner source)
links.outputNotes: Dispatched from ADAPT executor via outputType: "CALLER_ATTRIBUTE_NEXT" (added to enum by #2154). Pre-#2154 the runner was unreachable from the executor; PR #2153 shipped the runner standalone behind config.category: "session-focus-policy" discriminator on outputType=ADAPT specs. First instance of the substrate: IELTS-P3-FOCUS-001 spec (PR #2150 / epic #2145 S4) — projects CallerTarget.currentScore on the 4 IELTS skill params to one of 4 Part3TechniqueFocus labels. The written value is the LEARNER-facing label from spec.selectionRules (e.g. "giving reasons"), NEVER an internal parameter id. Generic runner — input skill ids are spec-config driven (per-course); consumesKey is scope-based to skip per-sample overlap check (samples are field names present in runner source).

links.consumesKey: CallerAttribute.key startsWith pattern 'session_focus:next_' (scope-based — generic transform for any course/module slug)
links.outputKey: [SESSION FOCUS] block in composed prompt + PinnedCardContent.focusArea projection
links.outputNotes: Returns null when no row exists (honest empty-state — no hardcoded fallback per feedback_no_hardcoded_score_backfill.md). Renderer push registered in coverage-producer-consumer.test.ts PAIRS manifest.
